summary: All 4 reviewers passed with no blocking issues. Plan is a well-scoped single-file refactoring with comprehensive existing test coverage. 3 actionable warnings consolidated from across reviewers, plus 2 recommendations.

warnings.recommendation: Remove the fallback note in Step 4. The existing file already uses `from sqlalchemy import and_, or_, select, union` at line 14 — add `CompoundSelect`, `ColumnElement`, `FromClause` to that same import. No secondary import path needed.

Replace `join_kwargs = dict(...)` / `**join_kwargs` with two direct calls passing all 5 keyword arguments explicitly. The 10 extra lines of repetition buy static analysis coverage — mypy can verify each keyword-only parameter is satisfied. This is the higher-priority warning.

warnings.issue: No isolated unit test for `_build_ref_union_query` helper
warnings.recommendation: Not a correctness risk — existing integration tests cover all 8 ref types through both code paths. Consider adding a focused test in a follow-up if the helper gains additional callers. Not required for this refactoring.

recommendations.suggestion: Note in commit message that `_find_affected_run_ids_chunk` has different-shaped duplication and is intentionally excluded from this helper extraction.

Step 7 (mypy) will catch any import issues, but confirm `pyproject.toml` SQLAlchemy version constraint includes 2.x where `CompoundSelect` is available at top-level. This is already confirmed by Reality reviewer — SQLAlchemy 2.x is in use and all three types are importable from `sqlalchemy`.

conflicts_resolved.issue: Import path for CompoundSelect, ColumnElement, FromClause
conflicts_resolved.reality_view: Top-level `from sqlalchemy import` works and matches existing file style
conflicts_resolved.architecture_view: Drop the fallback note, use `from sqlalchemy import` consistently
conflicts_resolved.quality_view: Verify importability (Step 7 mypy would catch it)
conflicts_resolved.resolution: All reviewers agree: use top-level import, drop the fallback note. Reality confirmed the symbols exist. Mypy in Step 7 is the safety net.
